children.heading: AIDS
children.label: 1 AIDS
children.text: The term “AIDS” means the acquired immune deficiency syndrome.

children.heading: Appropriate congressional committees
children.label: 2 Appropriate congressional committees
children.text: The term “appropriate congressional committees” means the Committee on Foreign Relations of the Senate and the Committee on Foreign Affairs of the House of Representatives, the Committee on Appropriations of the Senate, and the Committee on Appropriations of the House of Representatives.

children.heading: Global AIDS Coordinator
children.label: 3 Global AIDS Coordinator
children.text: The term “Global AIDS Coordinator” means the Coordinator of United States Government Activities to Combat HIV/AIDS Globally.

children.heading: Global Fund
children.label: 4 Global Fund
children.text: The term “Global Fund” means the public-private partnership known as the Global Fund to Fight AIDS, Tuberculosis and Malaria established pursuant to Article 80 of the Swiss Civil Code.

children.heading: HIV
children.label: 5 HIV
children.text: The term “HIV” means the human immunodeficiency virus, the pathogen that causes AIDS.

children.heading: HIV/AIDS
children.label: 6 HIV/AIDS
children.text: The term “HIV/AIDS” means, with respect to an individual, an individual who is infected with HIV or living with AIDS.

children.heading: Impact evaluation research
children.label: 7 Impact evaluation research
children.text: The term “impact evaluation research” means the application of research methods and statistical analysis to measure the extent to which change in a population-based outcome can be attributed to program intervention instead of other environmental factors.

children.heading: Operations research
children.label: 8 Operations research
children.text: The term “operations research” means the application of social science research methods, statistical analysis, and other appropriate scientific methods to judge, compare, and improve policies and program outcomes, from the earliest stages of defining and designing programs through their development and implementation, with the objective of the rapid dissemination of conclusions and concrete impact on programming.

children.heading: Paraprofessional
children.label: 9 Paraprofessional
children.text: The term “paraprofessional” means an individual who is trained and employed as a health agent for the provision of basic assistance in the identification, prevention, or treatment of illness or disability.

children.heading: Partner government
children.label: 10 Partner government
children.text: The term “partner government” means a government with which the United States is working to provide assistance to combat HIV/AIDS, tuberculosis, or malaria on behalf of people living within the jurisdiction of such government.

children.label: A
children.text: how well a program is carried out; and

children.heading: Relevant executive branch agencies
children.label: 12 Relevant executive branch agencies
children.text: The term “relevant executive branch agencies” means the Department of State, the United States Agency for International Development, and any other department or agency of the United States that participates in international HIV/AIDS activities pursuant to the authorities of such department or agency or the Foreign Assistance Act of 1961 [ et seq.].22 U.S.C. 2151
